evals.name: debug-broken-button
evals.prompt: My signup page is broken — the 'Sign Up' button does nothing when clicked. The page is at file://FIXTURES/signup.html (open it locally). Figure out what's wrong. Check the browser console for errors.
evals.expected_output: Identifies that clicking the button throws an uncaught ReferenceError because validateForm is not defined, found via the captured console errors.

evals.name: scrape-js-rendered-list
evals.prompt: I have a local product catalog page at file://FIXTURES/products.html. The products are rendered by JavaScript after the page loads, so the raw HTML source is empty. Open the page in a browser and give me each product name and its price as a list.
evals.expected_output: A list of all 5 products with their correct names and prices: Wireless Keyboard $49.99, Ergonomic Mouse $34.99, USB-C Hub $29.99, Mechanical Numpad $59.99, Monitor Light Bar $39.99. The result must come from executing JS (a plain fetch of the HTML source would return an empty container).

evals.name: snapshot-and-act
evals.prompt: Open the local page at file://FIXTURES/form.html. Take an accessibility snapshot to identify the form controls, then fill the email field with 'test@example.com' and change the plan dropdown to 'Pro'. After making both changes, confirm what values the fields now hold.
evals.expected_output: Uses the accessibility snapshot to locate the email input and plan select via @ref references, sets the email to 'test@example.com' and the plan to 'pro', then confirms both values are set correctly.

evals.name: multi-tab
evals.prompt: I want to compare two local pages side by side. Open file://FIXTURES/form.html in one tab and file://FIXTURES/dialog.html in a second tab. Tell me the page title and the main interactive elements on each tab.
evals.expected_output: Reports on two separate named tabs: tab 1 (form.html) has title 'Form Fixture' with an email input, a plan dropdown, a Save button, a disabled Locked button, and a Help link; tab 2 (dialog.html) has title 'Dialog Fixture' with a single 'Go' button.

evals.name: dialog-handling
evals.prompt: Open the page at file://FIXTURES/dialog.html. Click the 'Go' button — it will trigger a browser confirmation dialog. Accept the dialog and tell me what the page shows afterwards.
evals.expected_output: Clicks the Go button, detects the confirm() dialog with the prompt 'Proceed?', accepts it, and reports that the page now displays 'accepted' in the output area.
